title: BillMate Lite
one_line_vibe: A tiny bill splitter that turns shared expenses into clear per-person settlement suggestions.
current_idea: BillMate Lite is a small local Node.js domain utility for shared bills. Given expenses with a payer, amount, description, and participants, it calculates who should pay whom so the group can settle up with minimal transfers. The prototype intentionally stays small: no UI, no accounts, no cloud sync, and no persistence.

idea_expansion.problem: Shared expenses are easy to discuss casually but hard to settle cleanly without a small calculation step.
idea_expansion.core_value: Tell each person exactly whom to pay and how much.

idea_evolution.after: Create a tiny bill splitter as a scratch project to test VibeLog without needing the user to manually provide a real project.
idea_evolution.reason: The user asked whether the agent could simulate a small billing project instead of requiring manual testing.

idea_evolution.after: A Node.js settlement-calculation core with tests, no UI, and no persistence.
idea_evolution.reason: A small domain core is enough to exercise execution prompts, development logs, validation design, verification evidence, and handoff while keeping the scratch project disposable.

decisions.decision: Use a billing splitter as the simulated dogfood project.
decisions.why: It is small, concrete, and has enough logic to produce real tests and verification evidence.
decisions.impact: The test can prove VibeLog works with code artifacts while keeping app source outside the skill repository.

decisions.decision: Keep scratch app source outside the VibeLog repository.
decisions.why: The VibeLog repository is skill-first and should not become a mixed app/source monorepo.
decisions.impact: Only generated VibeLog files are suitable for copying into examples.

human_in_the_loop.human_input: 必须得我手动吗？你不可以模拟一个小项目，做一个账单啥的小项目去测试吗？
human_in_the_loop.agent_proposal: Simulate a small billing project in a scratch directory, run it like a real project, and use it to test VibeLog.
human_in_the_loop.final_decision: The test is agent-simulated. The human does not need to manually create the project.
human_in_the_loop.why_it_mattered: This tests whether VibeLog can support real agent-led workflow, which is central to the long-term product vision.
human_in_the_loop.impact: Slice 2.1 becomes an agent-simulated dogfood test instead of a human manual test.

open_questions: Should future examples use English, Chinese, or bilingual logs?
Should the next dogfood test include a deterministic Markdown-to-JSON exporter?
Should scratch project source remain disposable, or should a separate demo-source repository be created later?

implementation_status.current_state: The scratch BillMate Lite project has a tested Node.js calculation module and a generated VibeLog.
completed: Created this VibeLog Markdown and JSON.
in_progress: Copying generated logs into the main VibeLog repository as an example.
pending: Add examples/billmate-lite/ to the main VibeLog repository.
Update root VibeLog with this dogfood test result.
next_actions: Copy generated VibeLog files as example output only.
Commit the example locally if accepted.

success_criteria: A test-first scratch implementation can be represented in VibeLog.
A future agent can understand the billing idea, implementation status, test evidence, and next actions from the log.
The main repo receives only generated log files as an example.
manual_test_steps: Confirm the one-line vibe and current idea are clear.
Confirm execution prompt and development log are present.
Run npm test in the scratch directory.
Read handoff state as a new agent.
edge_cases: multiple expenses simplify into fewer settlement rows
regression_points: Do not produce settlements for invalid expenses.
Do not require UI or persistence for the core calculation.
Do not add scratch source code to the VibeLog skill repository.
risks_safety_privacy: No real financial data is used.
All names and expenses are fake.

verification_evidence.summary: Watched tests fail before implementation because src/bill-splitter.js did not exist.
verification_evidence.residual_risk: This proves the tests detected missing implementation, not final correctness.

verification_evidence.summary: Node test suite passed after implementing calculateSettlements.
verification_evidence.details: 3 tests passed: one shared bill split, multiple bill simplification, invalid expense rejection.
verification_evidence.residual_risk: This is a small domain test only. It does not cover currencies, unequal splits, UI, persistence, or payment flows.

known_issues: No deterministic Markdown-to-JSON exporter was used; Markdown and JSON were generated manually for this dogfood test.
do_not_change: Do not copy scratch app source into the VibeLog repository.
Do not treat this as a production billing product.

artifact_index.name: Scratch project source
artifact_index.notes: Local-only scratch source for dogfood validation. Not copied into the VibeLog skill repository.

execution_prompts.prompt_summary: User asked whether the agent could simulate a small billing project instead of requiring manual testing.
execution_prompts.prompt_text: 必须得我手动吗？你不可以模拟一个小项目，做一个账单啥的小项目去测试吗？
execution_prompts.result: Created a scratch BillMate Lite project, wrote tests first, implemented a small settlement calculator, ran verification, and generated VibeLog files.
execution_prompts.reuse_notes: Use agent-simulated scratch projects to dogfood VibeLog before asking the human to provide real unfinished work.

development_log.summary: Added failing tests for bill settlement behavior.
development_log.details: Added Node built-in test coverage for one shared bill, multiple bill simplification, and invalid expense rejection.
development_log.verification: npm test failed with ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND because implementation did not exist yet.

development_log.details: Added calculateSettlements, validation, cent-based equal splitting, and debtor/creditor settlement simplification.
follow_up: Add unequal split support only if a future user story needs it.

bugfix_incident_log.summary: Prevented invalid expenses from entering settlement calculation.
bugfix_incident_log.bug_symptom: An expense with zero amount or no participants would make the settlement output meaningless.
bugfix_incident_log.root_cause: Validation needed to be explicit before calculation.
bugfix_incident_log.fix: Added input validation for payer, positive amount, and non-empty participants.
bugfix_incident_log.verification: rejects invalid expenses before calculating settlements passed in npm test.
bugfix_incident_log.follow_up: Add validation for duplicate participant names if future inputs come from free-form UI.

handoff_state.current_state: BillMate Lite is a local scratch project with a tested bill splitting core. It exists only to dogfood VibeLog.
in_progress: Copying generated VibeLog files into the main VibeLog repository example directory.
pending: Update root VibeLog with this dogfood result.
next_actions: Copy vibe-log.md and vibe-log.json to examples/billmate-lite/.
Do not copy scratch source code.
Use this example to decide whether the next slice should be exporter automation.
context_for_next_agent: This scratch project is intentionally tiny.
The important artifact is the generated VibeLog, not the billing code.
The scratch source path is local and private.

public_private_projection.public_summary: BillMate Lite is a tiny local bill splitter used to dogfood VibeLog's process recording.

what_can_be_reused: the generated VibeLog example after the main repository license is chosen

vibe_progress.what_happened: Simulated a small billing project to dogfood VibeLog.
problems: Needed to test VibeLog without requiring the human to provide a real unfinished project.
next: Copy generated logs into the VibeLog repository as example output only.

public_summary: BillMate Lite is a tiny local bill splitter used to dogfood VibeLog. The example proves that VibeLog can record an agent-simulated project from idea through tests, implementation, verification, and handoff without requiring website features or app source in the skill repository.
